title: Erpham Warrick
content:
{{Top|ga|leg}} {{Character |type= |image=[[File:Erpham-WW.png]] |name=Erpham Warrick |homeworld=[[Endor/Legends|Endor]]{{Ewoks|Wicket's Wagon}} |birth= |death= |species=[[Ewok/Legends|Ewok]] |gender=[[Gender/Legends|Male]] |pronouns=He/him |height= |mass= |hair= |feathers= |eyes= |skin= |cyber= |families=[[Warrick family/Legends|Warrick family]] |parents= |partners= |siblings= |children= |affiliation=[[Bright Tree Village tribe]] |masters= |apprentices= }} {{Quote|Point is, you shouldn't quit once you put your mind to something.|Erpham Warrick|}} '''Erpham Warrick''' was an [[Ewok/Legends|Ewok]] [[Gender/Legends|male]] who created the [[Ewok battle wagon|battle wagon]], a machine the Ewoks used to [[First battle of the Soul Trees|defend themselves]] from the threat of [[Dulok/Legends|Duloks]]. Hundreds of [[Time/Legends|seasons]] later, in [[3 ABY/Legends|3 ABY]], Warrick's [[Warrick family/Legends|great-grandson]] [[Wicket Wystri Warrick/Legends|Wicket Warrick]] rebuilt the [[Wagon/Legends|wagon]], and Erpham gave him advice and encouragement on defeating the Duloks. ==Biography== {{Quote|Go get 'em, son.''"
"''I will, Great-grandfather.|Erpham and Wicket Warrick|}} Erpham Warrick was an [[Ewok/Legends|Ewok]] [[Gender/Legends|male]] who lived on the [[Endor/Legends|Forest Moon of Endor]] as part of the [[Bright Tree Village tribe]]. Hundreds of [[Time/Legends|seasons]] before [[3 ABY/Legends|3 ABY]],{{EwoksDate|the events|[[Wicket's Wagon]]}} the Ewoks' enemies, the [[Dulok/Legends|Duloks]], [[First battle of the Soul Trees|destroyed]] some of the Ewoks' precious [[Soul Tree]]s as part of the [[Ewok–Dulok wars/Legends|Ewok–Dulok wars]]. In response, Warrick created the [[Ewok battle wagon|battle wagon]], which he used to defend the Ewoks and drive away the Duloks. He came to be known as a legendary Ewok warrior.{{CSWECite|III|320|Warrick, Deej}} In 3 [[Galactic Standard Calendar|ABY]], Erpham's [[Warrick family/Legends|great-grandson]], [[Wicket Wystri Warrick/Legends|Wicket Wystri Warrick]]—son of [[Deej Warrick]], Erpham's grandson, and [[Shodu Warrick]], another of Erpham's descendants{{FactFile|1|WIC 1-4|Wicket W. Warrick}}—came across the battle [[Wagon/Legends|wagon's]] ruins and decided to fix it up with his friends after hearing about Erpham from the [[legend keeper]], [[Kaink/Legends|Kaink]]. Dulok [[Spy/Legends|spies]], watching, interpreted the move as a threat, so a few [[Standard week/Legends|weeks]] later, they [[Thief/Legends|stole]] the completed wagon for themselves. Unbeknownst to them, Wicket had been [[Sleep/Legends|sleeping]] in the contraption, but they forced him off once they realized they had a stowaway. Wicket ran back and awoke [[Bright Tree Village/Legends|Bright Tree Village]] to alert them, though all of the Ewoks were unconvinced that a wagon rebuilt by children would be a real concern. [[File:ErphamAdvice-WW.png|thumb|left|300px|Erpham Warrick (left) gives his grandson advice and wisdom.]] A dejected Wicket walked over to his great-grandfather's Soul Tree, where he apologized for making a mess of things. Erpham then appeared and spoke to Wicket, chiding him for apologizing for hard work. He revealed that when he had originally built the battle wagon, the Ewoks had laughed at him, too, until they realized its use against the Duloks. Erpham advised his great-grandson not to give up a task he had begun, and a newly invigorated Wicket ran off, ultimately saving the Ewoks by destroying the stolen battle wagon [[Standard second|seconds]] before it rammed into Erpham and his Soul Tree. Later, the village rebuilt the machine to thank Wicket, and Erpham's spirit winked at his descendant as the Ewoks honored Wicket. ==Personality and traits== {{Quote|You… you think it was easy for me to build that wagon with all those Ewoks gawking and laughing?|Erpham Warrick|}} Erpham Warrick stayed committed to his invention even as he was mocked, advising his great-grandson to similarly stick to what he believed was right. He had [[Color/Legends|blue-and-purple]] [[Hair/Legends|fur]] in his posthumous state. ==Behind the scenes== Erpham Warrick was created for "[[Wicket's Wagon]]," the tenth episode of the [[Ewoks: Season One|first season]] of the animated series [[Ewoks (television series)|''Ewoks'']], which aired on [[November 9]], [[1985]].{{InsiderCite|27|A Star Wars CELibration|A ''Star Wars'' CELibration}} He was voiced by [[Antony Parr]].{{BookCite|author=Perlmutter, David|title=The Encyclopedia of American Animated Television Shows|publisher=[[Wikipedia:Rowman & Littlefield|Rowman & Littlefield]]|date=2019-05-04|isbn=978-1538103739|link=https://archive.org/details/the-encyclopedia-of-american-animated-television-shows-perlmutter-david-z-lib.org}} {{C|Character name misspelled as "Erphram"}} The [[The Official Star Wars Fact File 84|eighty-fourth issue]] of [[De Agostini]]'s ''[[The Official Star Wars Fact File]]'' magazine,{{FactFile|84|LOG 1-2|Logray}} published around [[August 6]], [[2003]],{{FFdates|84}} and written by [[Iain Lowson]],{{LinkedIn|url=in/iainlowson|text=Iain Lowson|archivefile=File:IainLowson-LinkedIn.png}} mistakenly refers to Erpham as Wicket's grandfather instead of great-grandfather. ==Appearances== {{Mediacat|imagecat=Images of Erpham Warrick}} *{{Ewoks|Wicket's Wagon}} {{1st}} {{Ghost}} ==Sources== *''[[A Guide to the Star Wars Universe, Second Edition, Revised and Expanded]]'' *''[[The Essential Guide to Characters]]'' *''[[Star Wars Encyclopedia]]'' *''[[A Guide to the Star Wars Universe, Third Edition, Revised and Expanded]]'' *{{FactFile|1|WIC 1-4|Wicket W. Warrick}} *{{FactFile|84|LOG 1-2|Logray}} *''[[The Complete Star Wars Encyclopedia]]'' *{{StarshipsVehiclesCite|14|Droids, Aliens & Creatures}} *{{DB|character|deej|Warrick, Deej}} *{{DB|character|wicketwwarrick|Warrick, Wicket W.}} *{{SW|url=news/offscreen-but-not-forgotten-mini-rigs-body-rigs-and-other-kenner-vehicles|text=Offscreen, but Not Forgotten: Mini-Rigs, Body-Rigs, and Other Kenner Vehicles|int=Offscreen, but Not Forgotten: Mini-Rigs, Body-Rigs, and Other Kenner Vehicles|nolive=1}} *{{SW|url=news/meet-the-ewoks-from-endor|text=Yub Yub: Meet the Ewoks from Endor|int=Yub Yub: Meet the Ewoks from Endor|nolive=1}} *{{FactFile2014|45|WIC 1-4|Wicket W. Warrick}} ==Notes and references== {{Reflist}} {{Interlang |es=Erpham Warrick |nl=Erpham Warrick |pt=Erpham Warrick }} {{DEFAULTSORT:Warrick, Erpham}} [[Category:Bright Tree tribe]] [[Category:Ewoks]] [[Category:Individuals with he/him pronouns]] [[Category:Males]]
